codebook.title: My shift codes
codebook.duplicate: A code with this name already exists.

codebook.intro: Define the shift codes you use often, and entering shifts is as fast as picking a chip and tapping a date.

No codes yet.

Seed your common patterns to get started in one step.
codebook.emptyHint: Or add your own with the + button at the top right
codebook.returnPrompt: When you're done adding codes, head back to input
codebook.offCodeDesc: Shown as a day off, with no work hours
codebook.timeHint: Type just the digits and we'll format them as HH:MM. (e.g. 0930 → 09:30)
codebook.errorNameTooLong: Code name must be {max} characters or fewer.
codebook.errorTimeFormat: Enter the time in HH:MM format.

help.title: Help & terms
help.appDesc: An app for lining up work schedules with colleagues
help.faq1Q: Where do I get my schedule from?
help.faq1A: Upload the Excel (.xlsx) or CSV file from your company system as is, and it's recognized automatically. Photos and screenshots can be read via OCR too, but accuracy may be lower, so we recommend an Excel file when possible.
help.faq2Q: Can I hide my schedule from colleagues?
help.faq2A: In My info → Visibility, turn off the "Share my schedule with colleagues" toggle, and colleagues you've added to compare won't see your shift details.
help.faq3Q: How many colleagues can I add to compare?
help.faq3A: Up to 10. Any more and the calendar gets cluttered.
help.faq4Q: Does my schedule update automatically when it changes?
help.faq4A: It isn't linked to your company system, so you'll need to update it yourself. Re-add next month's schedule during the last week of each month.
help.contactDesc: Send us questions, bug reports, or suggestions
help.disclaimer: RaiLink is an independent app used freely by shift workers. It is not an official service of any airline or organization, and has no partnership or sponsorship with any company.

install.heroBody: RaiLink works like a regular app once you add it to your home screen — no separate install needed. Just set it up once.
install.iosStep2: Tap the Share button <chip></chip> at the bottom center.
install.iosStep3: Choose “Add to Home Screen” from the menu — that's it.
install.iosWarning: Be sure to open it in Safari, not Chrome. On iPhone, only Safari can add to the home screen.
install.androidStep2: Tap the <chip>Install app</chip> prompt that appears at the bottom or in the address bar.
install.androidStep3: If you don't see it, open the menu at the top right and choose “Install app” (or “Add to Home Screen”).
install.value1Head: Launch in one tap from your home screen
install.value1Sub: No address bar to go through.
install.value2Sub: The top and bottom bars in iOS Safari disappear.

public.title: Public
public.desc: Your name and photo appear in colleague search. Schedules still require separate acceptance.

private.title: Private
private.desc: You won't appear in search. Only colleagues who know your exact employee ID can request sharing.
